compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
rebar-debug Phase 1 (Gather) — dossier + leading hypothesis:

Claimed + working in worktree ../rebar-flaky-head off origin/main. This is a CHRONIC bug: prior fix edf7 (vocal-dip-robin, commit 2271912cc 'retry transient git object-DB failure on git add') confirmed the class as a transient runner-FS fault (git add failing to create a loose-object temp under the tracker's shared .git/objects/, ENOENT Linux / EINVAL macOS) and added a 3x retry — but ONLY on event_append._git_add (the CREATE path). The recurrence is on the TRANSITION path.

Leading hypothesis (to confirm): the transition/claim git ops run through _git() -> run_git_write() (src/rebar/_commands/txn.py:117-128, calls at 267-268 STATUS and 379-380 CLAIM). run_git_write (gitutil) self-heals ONLY git index.lock contention, NOT the transient object-DB signature that event_append._git_add retries. So the same transient FS fault that edf7 fixed for CREATE hits the transition path's 'git add'/'git commit' UNPROTECTED and surfaces as the hard StoreError 'git operation failed: fatal: could not parse HEAD' (exit 2, txn.py:128). i.e. edf7's fix was incomplete — it left the txn.py transition/claim path un-retried. NOTE edf7 wrongly assumed 'xdist absent -> serial'; CI runs -n 4 --dist worksteal.

Running now: 3 parallel investigators (code-tracer: exact git mechanism for 'could not parse HEAD' + retry-gap; concurrency/empirical: any hidden concurrency or shared object-DB across xdist workers, unborn-HEAD timing, gc; historical: edf7 fix scope + full git-op call-site retry-coverage table + sibling unprotected sites) + a best-effort local repro loop (edf7 could NOT repro locally: 0/50 isolated, 0/200 saturated, 6/6 clean full-suite — so a local RED may be infeasible).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
rebar-debug Phase 1 COMPLETE — root cause CONFIRMED (3 independent investigators converged + runtime oracle):

MECHANISM (proven by scratch-repo experiment, code-tracer): 'rebar transition' runs 'git commit' at txn.py:268 (STATUS) / 380 (CLAIM). git commit reads HEAD to set the new commit's parent (parse_commit). When HEAD's commit OBJECT is transiently unreadable/missing from the shared .git/objects/, git commit dies with EXACTLY 'fatal: could not parse HEAD' (exit 128); git add does NOT (returns 0). Reproduced verbatim by hiding HEAD's loose object then committing. Ruled out: unborn HEAD (-> root commit, exit 0), truncated HEAD (-> 'not a git repository'). HEAD is always born by transition time (init.py:372-380 commits before the fixture yields). Same runner-FS transient-loose-object fault edf7 fixed on the WRITE side (git add), now on the READ side (git commit parent lookup).

NO CONCURRENCY needed/active (concurrency investigator): async-push gated off (sync.push=always default + no remote), detached enrich-drain gated off (overlap_enabled=False default), repos triple-isolated per xdist worker/test, gc doesn't trigger at ~150-200 loose objs « 6700. (Caveat: gc.autoDetach=true COULD race on the large prod tracker -> same signature as sibling bug a16d; not this isolated test.)

ROOT CAUSE (changeable artifact): the transition/claim commit routes through run_git_write -> _with_index_lock_retry (gitutil.py) which retries ONLY index.lock. 'could not parse HEAD' is retried NOWHERE. edf7's fix is doubly out of reach: wrong helper (only event_append._git_add) AND wrong git verb (only add, not commit). LEGITIMACY = defect, cited to the self-heal contract at event_append.py:164-172 ('a transient filesystem hiccup, NOT a data fault ... Retrying ONLY this signature turns a runner-FS blip ... into a self-healed write') and the edf7 comment at :355-359 that named 'could not parse HEAD' but never retried it.

FIX SHAPE (idiomatic, precedented 2x): extend the bounded-retry-on-transient-stderr pattern — add the commit/ref-read signature ('could not parse HEAD' + object-read tree-build siblings) to a transient vocabulary and wrap the COMMIT step in the same retry, centralized on the shared run_git_write/gitutil seam so transition/claim (and create) commits are covered. RED test injects 'fatal: could not parse HEAD' on a transition's first commit and asserts self-heal, mirroring tests/interfaces/store/test_event_append_transient_retry.py. Local repro infeasible (edf7 0/275+, my loop clean) -> RED oracle is injection-based. Moving to Phase 2 (RED->GREEN). Sibling unprotected sites (Step-7 sweep): event_append._git_commit/_git_commit_paths, delete.py, compact.py commits.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
rebar-debug Phase 2 (RED->GREEN) in progress:

RED ORACLE CONFIRMED: tests/interfaces/store/test_txn_transient_retry.py injects 'fatal: could not parse HEAD' on the FIRST transition git commit (monkeypatching gitutil.subprocess.run) and asserts self-heal. Before any fix it FAILS with the EXACT reported error: 'rebar._errors.RebarError: rebar transition failed (exit 2): Error: git operation failed: fatal: could not parse HEAD' at _lib_writes.py:358 — same signature as CI run 29470200567. Negative test (non-transient commit failure surfaces immediately, not retried) passes. RED for the right reason.

FIX (blast radius confirmed): txn.py:_git, delete.py:176, compact.py:59 ALL route through gitutil.run_git_write -> centralizing a transient retry there fixes 3 sibling families at once. event_append._git_commit/_git_commit_paths use _with_index_lock_retry directly -> covered in the same change. Test held out; fix implemented by an independent subagent from the root-cause + contract card (not the test). Design: add _with_transient_retry + _is_transient_git_error (marker 'could not parse head') to gitutil, composed as the INNER loop inside run_git_write (index.lock retry stays OUTER), mirroring the established _with_index_lock_retry + event_append transient-add-retry patterns (both tested). Next: restore held-out test, validate GREEN + mutation check (re-seed the root cause -> test back to RED).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Cross-session corroboration (independent /rebar-debug run, session != e0317d38): I
reached the SAME confirmed root cause independently — `git commit` in the transition
write path (txn.py:268 via run_git_write) reads HEAD to set the new commit's parent;
when HEAD's commit object is transiently unreadable on a CI runner FS it dies with
exactly `fatal: could not parse HEAD` (exit 128), while `git add` does not. Same
runner-FS transient-loose-object class as edf7 (commit 2271912cc), but on the READ
side; run_git_write self-heals only index.lock, not this signature. Fix direction
(centralize a signature-scoped transient retry in gitutil.run_git_write, covering
txn/delete/compact + event_append commit) matches — agreed.

One extra data point: I stress-ran the exact heavy sequence 480× across 12 parallel
workers WITH forced aggressive gc (gc.auto=1, autoDetach=true) on macOS and got ZERO
reproductions — consistent with this being a runner-FS transient (not a background-gc
ref race), and supports the deterministic-injection RED oracle over trying to race it.

Deferring to this session (e0317d38), which is ahead in Phase 2 — NOT pushing a
competing change to avoid a duplicate. Flagging only in case this session has stalled;
if so I can carry the confirmed fix to Gerrit.

compiled_state.description:
The unit test `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_transition_open_children.py::test_guard_counts_only_direct_children_not_unrelated`
fails INTERMITTENTLY in CI with `rebar._errors.RebarError: rebar transition failed
(exit 2): Error: git operation failed: fatal: could not parse HEAD`. It is NOT a
CI-vs-Gerrit drift (Gerrit's Verified gate runs the identical pytest matrix, so it
fails there too) — it is test-isolation / concurrency flakiness under pytest-xdist
(`-n 4 --dist worksteal`), where a temp git store is exercised before its initial
commit exists (unborn HEAD), so a `rebar transition` git op hits `could not parse HEAD`.

Discovered while resolving bug `pillared-doddering-fawn` (the deterministic
`no-commit-to-branch` CI-drift red). Filed separately because it is a different
failure CLASS (flaky test, not drift) and out of scope for that change.

## Evidence
- run 29470200567: FAILED on cell `pytest (ubuntu-latest, py3.12)` only (py3.11,
  py3.13, macOS all PASSED). `1 failed, 6171 passed` — the single failure is this test.
- run 29468054945: FAILED on cell `pytest (ubuntu-latest, py3.13)` instead; py3.12
  PASSED that run. i.e. the failing cell MOVES run-to-run → non-deterministic.
- Failing assertion context: the test drives `rebar transition` on a store with
  child tickets; the git op raises `fatal: could not parse HEAD`.

## Reproduction Steps
- [ ] Run the interface suite under xdist repeatedly:
      `pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_transition_open_children.py -n 4 --dist worksteal -q`
      (and/or the full interface tier) enough times to surface the intermittent failure.
- [ ] Observe an occasional `git operation failed: fatal: could not parse HEAD` from
      `rebar transition` in `test_guard_counts_only_direct_children_not_unrelated`.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: the test passes deterministically on every cell / every run (a fresh test
  store always has a parseable HEAD before a transition git op runs).
- Actual: it fails on ~1 random matrix cell per run with `could not parse HEAD`,
  reddening branch CI (and it would redden the Verified gate too).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root-cause the `could not parse HEAD` (the test-fixture git-store setup that
      leaves HEAD unborn / races the initial commit under xdist worker parallelism),
      cited to the fixture/code (`path:line`).
- [ ] Fix so the test store always has a committed HEAD before any `rebar transition`
      git op, under xdist parallelism (e.g. ensure the fixture commits an initial
      state / is isolated per worker).
- [ ] Demonstrate stability: the test passes across repeated xdist runs (quantified,
      e.g. 50/50 green) — a single green run does not close a flake.
- [ ] `make test` (or the interface tier) green on all matrix cells.
compiled_state.title: Flaky test: test_guard_counts_only_direct_children_not_unrelated fails intermittently with 'could not parse HEAD' under xdist
